ddls:
CREATE TABLE "boroughs" (
  "borough_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each borough */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the borough */,
  "borough_seat" TEXT /* Seat of the borough (e.g., Sand Point, Anchorage) */,
  "fips_code" INTEGER /* FIPS code for the borough */,
  "class" INTEGER /* ID of the classification of the borough */,
  "established" TEXT /* Date the borough was established */,
  "website" TEXT /* URL to the official borough website */,
  "contact_info" TEXT /* Contact details for the borough administration */,
  "mayor_name" TEXT /* Name of the current mayor or administrative head */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("borough_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_boroughs_class FOREIGN KEY ("class") REFERENCES borough_classes ("class_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "borough_boundaries" (
  "boundary_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each boundary */,
  "borough_id" INTEGER /* ID of the borough the boundary belongs to */,
  "geometry" TEXT /* Geographic boundary of the borough (in a format such as WKT or GeoJSON) */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the boundary */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the boundary data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("boundary_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_borough_boundaries_borough_id FOREIGN KEY ("borough_id") REFERENCES boroughs ("borough_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "demographics" (
  "demographic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each demographic entry */,
  "borough_id" INTEGER /* ID of the borough the demographic data belongs to */,
  "population_density" REAL /* Population density of the borough */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the borough */,
  "area_sq_mi" REAL /* Area of the borough in square miles */,
  "area_km2" REAL /* Area of the borough in square kilometers */,
  "median_age" REAL /* Median age of the population */,
  "median_income" REAL /* Median household income */,
  "education_level" TEXT /* Percentage of the population with different education levels */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("demographic_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_demographics_borough_id FOREIGN KEY ("borough_id") REFERENCES boroughs ("borough_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "borough_classes" (
  "class_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each class */,
  "class_name" TEXT /* Name of the class (e.g., Second, Unified Home Rule, Home Rule) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the class */,
  "legislation_reference" TEXT /* Reference to the legislation defining the class */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("class_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., administrator, researcher, public) */,
  "full_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user the permission belongs to */,
  "borough_id" INTEGER /* ID of the borough the permission is for */,
  "permission" TEXT /* Permission level (e.g., read, write, admin) */,
  "permission_detail" TEXT /* Detailed description of the permission */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_permissions_borough_id FOREIGN KEY ("borough_id") REFERENCES boroughs ("borough_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_permissions_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "activity_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who performed the activity */,
  "borough_id" INTEGER /* ID of the borough the activity was for */,
  "activity" TEXT /* Type of activity (e.g., view, edit, delete) */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the activity */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the activity was performed */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent of the browser used for the activity */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_activity_logs_borough_id FOREIGN KEY ("borough_id") REFERENCES boroughs ("borough_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_activity_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
